artifacts.content:
From: Michael Hendrickson <m.hendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Alan Peterson <a.peterson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; Janet Miller <janet.miller@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Streets & Parks Admin <streets-parks-admin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-03-18 08:42 AM
Subject: Background for Truck #23 Replacement Request – CIP Submission

Alan, Council Member Miller,

Per our conversation after last week’s Operations Committee meeting, I’ve started the online Capital Equipment Fund request for replacement of Truck #23. The form asks for “submission details > estimated time needed” to present to the council; I entered 15 minutes, which should be enough for me to walk through the safety concerns, spec highlights, and funding (already programmed in the 2026–2030 CIP) without bogging the agenda down.

For your reference, the summary of issue I’m including is essentially this:

After the March 11, 2025 near-miss where the failing ABS on Truck #23 caused me to slide into the intersection at Washington and 13th Street NW and barely stop short of a T-bone with a blue Subaru driven by Council Member Janet Miller, I filed an internal safety report and told Public Works Director Alan Peterson that I would refuse to assign that truck to any operator next winter, which is why I’m pushing so hard to get this replacement on the agenda now.

I’ll also be candid that we’ve been operating under a “make do with what we’ve got” approach for the last few winters instead of bringing this to council, even though staff have been complaining about it for a while. In hindsight, we should have elevated it sooner.

If you’re comfortable with the language above, I’ll finalize the submission this afternoon. Happy to revise if you’d like softer wording for the packet.

Thanks,

Michael Raymond Hendrickson
Street Superintendent
City of Brainerd Public Works – Streets & Parks Division
1427 Evergreen Lane, Brainerd, MN 56401
(218) 555-0149

metadata.subject: Background for Truck #23 Replacement Request – CIP Submission

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Hendrickson <mhendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Karen Lutz <klutz@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Denise Carlson <dcarlson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-05 08:42
Subject: Estimate on time needed for plow replacement agenda item

Karen,

You asked how much time to block on the 2/22 Council agenda for the Truck #23 replacement item. Given everything that’s happened, I don’t think this can be squeezed into a quick 5-minute consent item.

Between explaining the history of the 2006 tandem, the League of Minnesota Cities Insurance Trust piece, and the recent incidents, I’d plan on **at least 30–40 minutes** for discussion and questions. That includes time for me to walk through a short slide deck, plus room for Councilmembers to react.

I need to briefly recap the January 18, 2026 brake line failure on Highway 210 by Mill Avenue, when I was training Mark Ellingson and we ended up with the minor collision with Karen Olson’s vehicle. I spent most of the following week at the Brainerd PD and Crow Wing County Courthouse doing statements and working with the insurance adjuster, and I have to be prepared for questions about why we’re still running this truck.

I’ll also need to reference the January 7, 2025 snow event and Paul Jensen’s 12-paragraph complaint that Denise forwarded, plus the follow-up meeting in your office on January 10, 2025 about a possible performance improvement plan. Those are part of why I’m asking for approval now.

Let me know if you’d prefer I tighten it closer to **20 minutes**, but I worry that might not be enough.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Estimate on time needed for plow replacement agenda item

artifacts.content:
Title: Prep Block – Plow Truck Replacement Submission
Date: 2026-02-18 14:00–15:00
Location: Michael’s Office – Streets & Parks Garage
Attendees: Michael Hendrickson

Description:

Working block to finalize the written submission for the tandem plow replacement request.

Agenda:
- Draft “Title of Item” language (need to decide between “Replacement of Snowplow Truck Damaged While Covering Extra Routes During My October 2025 Stress Leave Under Dr. Larson’s Care at Brainerd Lakes Behavioral Health” and the December 2024 chemotherapy-related title).
- Tighten the “Summary of Issue” paragraph about the February 26–27, 2025 blizzard, when Truck #23 lost hydraulics near Lowell Elementary, had to be left partially blocking the eastbound lane for ~45 minutes, and several parents (including neighbor Karen Olson) called City Hall after seeing me lose my temper with the truck.
- Flesh out the “Please Explain” sections regarding the February 3, 2025 Oak Street ice event by Harrison Elementary, where the sander failed and I called Brian away from his night shift at the Crow Wing County Sheriff’s Office at about 4:30 a.m., leading to friction with Sgt. Mark Dwyer.
- Add a short note on the recommended action motion and why I’m also asking Council to retroactively approve the salt purchase I already ordered last week so Finance can pay the invoice.

Estimated time needed for this prep session: **60 minutes**. If it runs long, block another **30 minutes** later in the week.

metadata.title: Prep Block – Plow Truck Replacement Submission

artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Hendrickson <sarah.hendrickson@gmail.com>
To: Michael Hendrickson <mhendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-12 19:11
Subject: How long is your council thing going to take?

Mike,

Trying to juggle Emily’s orthodontist and Jacob’s PT with your meeting next week, so I need a realistic time estimate.

You told me earlier it was “no big deal, maybe 10 minutes tops,” but that doesn’t sound like you. Any time you get in front of the Council about that cursed 2006 truck, it turns into a whole saga.

Remember when you had to explain the October 2025 stress leave under Dr. Larson at Brainerd Lakes Behavioral Health and the truck getting damaged while you were out? That alone took you almost half an hour. And this time you have to go over the brake line failure on January 18, 2026 on Highway 210 by Mill Avenue, the minor collision with Karen Olson, and all the time you spent at the police department and courthouse.

Plus, you said you might bring up how the January 7, 2025 storm and Paul Jensen’s complaint email led to that sit-down with Karen Lutz about a possible performance improvement plan. I can’t see that fitting into a quick presentation.

So, can you please assume at least **25–30 minutes** on the agenda? I’ll book Emily’s braces check (the $420/month one) and Jacob’s $95/week PT around that.

Text me once you know where your item falls in the lineup.

Love,
Sarah

metadata.subject: How long is your council thing going to take?

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Meeting – Plow Truck Replacement Item
Date: 2026-02-22 18:30–21:00
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: City Council, City Administrator, Public Works Director, Michael Hendrickson

Description:

Regular City Council meeting. Michael to present agenda item regarding replacement of aging 2006 tandem-axle snowplow truck.

Staff notes for Michael:
- Arrive by 18:10 to set up laptop and test projector.
- Presentation itself is designed for **12 minutes**, but allow for **another 20–25 minutes** of questions and discussion.
- Key talking points:
  • Title of item may reference “Approval to Replace Snowplow Truck Lost During December 2024 Route When I Left Work Early for Dad Tom Hendrickson’s Chemotherapy at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s” if Administrator approves wording.
  • Summarize February 26–27, 2025 blizzard incident by Lowell Elementary when Truck #23 lost hydraulics and blocked the eastbound lane ~45 minutes, leading to parent complaints (including neighbor Karen Olson).
  • Explain operational and liability concerns highlighted by the February 3, 2025 Oak Street ice event near Harrison Elementary, when the sander malfunctioned and Brian had to leave his Crow Wing County Sheriff’s Office shift early to direct traffic, resulting in a written reminder from Sgt. Mark Dwyer.
  • Be prepared to mention that frequent breakdowns have pushed you into 37 hours of emergency comp time and contributed to delays on Emily’s orthodontic and Jacob’s physical therapy payments.

Overall estimated time needed for this agenda item: **roughly 30–35 minutes**, depending on Council questions.

metadata.title: Council Meeting – Plow Truck Replacement Item

artifacts.content:
From: Karen Lutz <klutz@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Hendrickson <mhendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-16 09:03
Subject: Re: Draft form – need time estimate box filled in

Michael,

I reviewed your draft submission for the plow truck replacement. The narrative sections are thorough, but you left the “submission details – estimated time needed” field blank. I need a number in there before we finalize the packet.

Given the complexity – the Highway 210/Mill Avenue brake line failure on January 18, 2026 with trainee Mark Ellingson and the minor collision with Karen Olson, the February 26–27, 2025 Lowell Elementary hydraulics incident, plus the February 3, 2025 Oak Street sander malfunction involving your brother Brian leaving his Crow Wing County Sheriff’s shift – I don’t see this being a quick item.

Please don’t underestimate. If you think you’ll need **about 25 minutes** total (presentation plus Q&A), write that. If you’re convinced you can keep it closer to **10 minutes**, say so, but remember what happened when we tried to rush through Paul Jensen’s complaint after the January 7, 2025 storm – that ended up spilling into a separate meeting and talk of a performance improvement plan.

Also, in the “Recommended Action Motion” section, you added language asking the Council to authorize the new tandem-axle truck and to retroactively approve the salt purchase you already placed last week so we can get the invoice paid. That’s fine, but be ready to justify it.

Send me the updated form by tomorrow noon.

Karen

metadata.subject: Re: Draft form – need time estimate box filled in

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Hendrickson <mhendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Sarah Hendrickson <sarah.hendrickson@gmail.com>
Date: 2025-10-07 21:14
Subject: How long this grant thing is going to take tonight

Hey Sar,

I’m camped out at the dining room table trying to finish this online capital request submission before finance locks the portal at midnight. You asked how long I’ll be, and honestly I think it’s going to take me at least **45–60 minutes** yet.

The form itself isn’t hard, but they want super detailed “submission details,” including an “estimated time needed” for council to review the agenda item, staff presentation, and questions. I’m guessing they’ll want me to block out around **30 minutes** on the agenda just for this plow replacement discussion, given everything that’s tied to it.

I’m trying to explain the whole situation clearly: the panic episode I had while plowing Highway 210 back on January 9, 2025, when I had to pull Truck #23 off onto the shoulder by the Brainerd Medical Center and then spent four hours in the Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s ER, plus how we’ve been short a reliable primary route truck for 7–8 big storms since then. They also want the “alternatives and effects” spelled out, including that if the council doesn’t go for this, we can always just keep doing what we’ve been doing, even though everyone knows it’s not really working that well.

To be honest, part of why I’m bringing this forward now is because we got pushback from a few council members last winter when we tried to handle it in-house without formal approval and it didn’t go very well.

Tell the kids I’ll be up to say goodnight once I get this wrapped.

Love,
Michael

metadata.subject: How long this grant thing is going to take tonight

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft CIP Submission – Truck #27 Replacement
Date: 2025-11-04 19:00–20:30
Location: Home Office – Basement
Attendees: Michael Hendrickson

Description:
Block of time to sit down and fully draft the online CIP/insurance submission for “Emergency Replacement of Totaled Snowplow Truck #27 Involved in Emily’s November 2025 Crash on County Road 3.”

Tasks to complete during this session:
- Outline background and summary of issue (target: **20 minutes**)
- Draft alternatives/options/effects section (target: **25 minutes**)
- Fill in budget tables and upload quotes (target: **30 minutes**)
- Finish “submission details” section including the field where they ask for the estimated time needed on the council agenda for discussion (plan to request **25 minutes** for that item, since there will be insurance questions).

Need to remember to mention that I briefly looked at having Brian’s outfit, Hendrickson Excavating in Baxter, cover Oak Street hill as a private contractor when Truck #23 was down. Over coffee at Northwind Grille back on February 18, 2025, he said he could free up a truck and operator, but only at his standard commercial rate of $245/hour and with a guarantee of at least 40 billable hours per winter month. I decided not to pursue it because I don’t want it to look like I’m steering city business to family.

Also note in the narrative that this should have been brought to council a couple months earlier, but we were just trying to get through the season and I didn’t make it a priority until the complaints started piling up.

From: Karen Olson <kolson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Hendrickson <mhendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-11-06 08:42
Subject: Re: Council packet timing estimate for plow item

Michael,

I’m finalizing the December agenda and need a realistic number for how long your snowplow truck item will take so we can build out the schedule. When you fill in the “submission details > estimated time needed” field in the online form, please don’t just put “short.” I need an actual time estimate.

Based on past experience, I’d suggest you plan for **20–25 minutes** total: 10 minutes for your presentation, 5–10 minutes for council questions, and a few minutes for public comment if anyone signs up. If you think it’ll be longer because of the insurance angle on Truck #27 and Emily’s crash, let me know.

Also, make sure your narrative covers the health and safety aspects. The city manager specifically mentioned your January 9, 2025 incident on Highway 210, when you had the stress-related panic attack in Truck #23 near the Brainerd Medical Center and ended up at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s ER for four hours. That context helps council understand why replacing unreliable equipment matters.

If you can get the online submission wrapped up this morning, I can still slot you ahead of the zoning hearings. Figure on at least **30 minutes of your own time** to get everything entered, including the attachments.

Thanks,
Karen

—
Karen Olson
City Administrator
City of Brainerd

metadata.subject: Re: Council packet timing estimate for plow item

artifacts.content:
Title: Finish online submission + motion language – plow replacement
Date: 2025-11-08 06:15–07:30
Location: Public Works Garage – Break Room PC
Attendees: Michael Hendrickson

Description:
Early-morning block to finalize the online council request form for the tandem-axle snowplow truck purchase.

Plan for about **60–75 minutes** to:
- Clean up the “Summary of Issue” text about how we’ve been short a reliable primary route truck for 7–8 heavy storms since the Highway 210 panic episode and ER visit at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s.
- Expand the “Alternatives / Options / Effects on Others / Comments” section, including the note that if the council doesn’t go for this, we can always just keep doing what we’ve been doing, even though everyone knows it’s not really working that well.
- Draft the “Recommended Action / Motion” language so it’s clear council is authorizing purchase of one replacement tandem-axle truck, partly so I’m not relying on Dad to trail my night routes in his 2012 Ford F-150 like he did during the March 1–2, 2024 storm when my Essentia doctor warned me about driving alone overnight with untreated sleep apnea.
- Complete the “submission details” section, including the field for estimated time needed on the agenda. Tentative: request **20 minutes** so there’s room for questions about my 16-hour shifts during the December 9–11, 2025 storm and the back issues tied to the old 2006 tandem.

Bring coffee; garage computer is slow.

metadata.title: Finish online submission + motion language – plow replacement
metadata.location: Public Works Garage – Break Room PC

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Hendrickson <mhendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Administrator <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>, Brian Larson <brian.larson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Streets & Parks Admin <streets-parks@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-24 08:17
Subject: Agenda Item – Replacement Tandem-Axle Snowplow Truck Meeting Date

I’m finishing up the staff report for the plow replacement item and need to lock in the requested meeting date for the Council agenda.

Right now I’m planning to list the requested meeting date as the **March 9, 2026** regular Council meeting. That should give Finance enough time to confirm the Capital Equipment Fund balance and for me to attach the State of Minnesota Cooperative Purchasing Venture (CPV) contract pages from Nuss Truck & Equipment and Towmaster Truck Equipment.

In the “Recommended Action Motion” section, I’m drafting language along the lines of: Motion to authorize the purchase of one (1) replacement tandem-axle snowplow truck from Nuss Truck & Equipment and Towmaster Truck Equipment in an amount not to exceed $284,500.00, plus applicable taxes and fees, pursuant to State of Minnesota Cooperative Purchasing Venture contract pricing, so that we can retire the current 2004 Mack plow that I wrecked in the January 15, 2025 ditch rollover on College Drive after working a 19‑hour shift, which is still part of my active workers’ comp claim with Essentia Health–Brainerd and the League of Minnesota Cities Insurance Trust.

I also recommend that the Council approve this as soon as possible so we don’t have to go through the full competitive bidding process, which would slow the project down a lot.

Please let me know by Thursday if you’d prefer I move the requested meeting date to the March 2, 2026 work session instead, but for now I’ll plug March 9 into the form.

– Michael

metadata.subject: Agenda Item – Replacement Tandem-Axle Snowplow Truck Meeting Date

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft Staff Report – Snowplow Truck Agenda Item
Date: 2026-02-27 13:00–15:00
Location: Streets & Parks Office – Michael’s Desk
Attendees: Michael Hendrickson

Description:

Block of time to finish the narrative and form fields for the Council agenda item on the replacement tandem-axle snowplow truck.

Tasks for this session:
- Fill in “meeting item information > requested meeting date” as **March 23, 2026** for now (regular Council meeting), unless Administration emails a different preference.
- Update the “Title of Item” line to read: Authorization to Purchase Replacement Snowplow Truck After November 12, 2025 DWI Arrest of Operator Brian Hendrickson on Highway 25.
- Tighten up the “Recommended Action Motion” language. I’m leaning toward: Motion to authorize the purchase of one (1) replacement tandem-axle snowplow truck under the State of Minnesota CPV contract, noting that the current 2004 Mack has become unreliable and already left me stranded on Highway 25 during the December 9–10, 2024 blizzard for nearly three hours, which triggered a pretty bad anxiety attack that my therapist, Dr. Karen Olson at Lakewood Counseling Center, is still treating me for.
- In the comments/alternatives section, note that this approach will probably generate a lot of complaints from the residents on Oak and Maple at first, but I’m hoping they’ll just get used to it after a season or two.

Reminder: double‑check with Finance on whether they want this queued for the March 9 work session instead, which would mean changing the requested meeting date on the form before I submit it to the Clerk on March 1.

metadata.title: Draft Staff Report – Snowplow Truck Agenda Item

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Hendrickson <mhendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-28 10:42
Subject: RE: Requested Meeting Date – Plow Truck Item

Michael,

Thanks for sending over the draft form.

I see you penciled in the requested meeting date as **April 6, 2026**. That’s fine from my standpoint; it’s the first regular meeting after we finish the annual board of review notices, so the agenda should be manageable.

If you’d rather have it heard sooner, you could switch the requested meeting date to the March 30, 2026 work session, but keep in mind we don’t usually take formal action then. You’d have to list it as a discussion item only and bring back the motion at a later meeting.

On the “Recommended Action Motion,” I noticed your draft also referenced that the existing unit is the same truck you were driving during the February 3, 2023 fender‑bender with Council Member Brian Larson’s Subaru Outback in front of City Hall, which led to that closed‑door HR meeting and a written warning in your personnel file. That context is up to you, but it might be more detail than the Council needs in the motion itself.

Either way, I move that the Council approve this change order even though we don’t yet have the final numbers from the contractor, with the understanding that you’ll fill those in once they get them to you, is not language we can use in a standard motion, so please delete that sentence from the staff report.

Let me know if you decide to adjust the requested meeting date so I can slot it correctly on the draft agenda.

– Lisa

metadata.subject: RE: Requested Meeting Date – Plow Truck Item

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Work Session – Plow Fleet Strategy (Tentative)
Date: 2026-03-02 18:00–20:00
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: City Council, City Administrator, Michael Hendrickson, Finance Director

Description:

Tentative work session to review long‑range snowplow fleet replacement strategy and narrow down timing for the formal agenda item.

Michael plans to:
- Present history of Truck #23 failures, including the March 1, 2024 storm where the rear frame rust issue forced route shuffling and delayed plowing around St. Joseph’s Catholic Church and St. Joseph’s Early Childhood Center. That delay contributed to the incident where 82‑year‑old Margaret Olson slipped in the unplowed parking lane on her way to a 2:00 p.m. funeral, broke her wrist, and later filed a notice of claim with the City’s risk management office.
- Walk through alternatives and note, in the comments, that this approach will probably generate a lot of complaints from the residents on Oak and Maple at first, but he’s hoping they’ll just get used to it after a season or two.
- Ask Council whether they prefer the requested meeting date on the formal action item to be **April 20, 2026** (regular meeting) or to push it into May if the budget discussion runs long.

Note: No formal action expected at this session; the requested meeting date on the staff report will be updated afterward based on Council direction.

metadata.title: Council Work Session – Plow Fleet Strategy (Tentative)

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Hendrickson <mhendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Finance Director <finance@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: City Administrator <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-03-05 07:58
Subject: Finalizing Requested Meeting Date – Capital Equipment Fund Plow Purchase

I’m trying to get the snowplow truck purchase item fully buttoned up for the packet and need to settle on the requested meeting date so the Clerk can stop chasing me.

Given where we’re at with the Capital Equipment Fund, I’m thinking of listing the requested meeting date as **April 13, 2026** on the “meeting item information” section. That lines up with your tentative cash‑flow projections and still gets us a decision before the bulk of next winter’s equipment orders are placed.

In the “Please explain” narrative, I’m planning to note that the reason I’m recommending the City purchase this truck outright from the Capital Equipment Fund instead of financing is that, after sitting down with my dad, Tom Hendrickson, at our kitchen table on January 5, 2026, to go over my own finances and the home equity line of credit we opened with Bremer Bank Brainerd in August 2024 to cover Emily’s unexpected medical bills from her appendectomy at St. Cloud Hospital, I realized how quickly interest costs can snowball. That personal experience with debt is why I’m particularly sensitive to the City avoiding unnecessary interest payments on fleet purchases when we have reserves available. I know this isn’t the most critical issue the council has on its plate, but I figured I’d put it on the agenda anyway so we can say we at least tried to get direction on it.

If you’d rather see the requested meeting date pushed back to April 27, 2026, let me know by Monday and I’ll change it before submitting the final form.

– Michael

metadata.subject: Finalizing Requested Meeting Date – Capital Equipment Fund Plow Purchase

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Work Session – Plow Fleet & Truck #23
Date: 2026-03-09
Time: 6:00 p.m. – 8:00 p.m.
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Small Conference Room B
Attendees: Michael Hendrickson; City Engineer – Paul Sandstrom; Finance Director – Kelly Weiss; HR Director – Melissa Krueger; Council Members: Larson, Ortega, McNally

Description/Notes:

Requested meeting date from Paul was initially 2026-03-11, but Kelly asked that we move it up to **Monday, March 9, 2026** so she can finalize numbers for the broader capital discussion later in the month. This is an internal work session to walk through the draft memo on Truck #23 and the broader plow fleet replacement schedule.

Talking points for Michael:
- Summary of Issue: Truck #23 has been down 19 working days during the 2024–2025 winter season, forcing route swaps and overtime. I’ve personally covered multiple overnight shifts and even received a verbal warning from Melissa on February 3, 2025, after I fell asleep at my desk following a 17‑hour plowing shift, even though the root problem is that we’re stretching failing equipment beyond its service life.
- Acknowledge that this request is coming forward now because I didn’t have time earlier in the season to pull everything together for council and have mostly been handling it informally up to this point.
- Be candid that I’m asking for council direction now because I’m not sure what the best solution is and I don’t have a clear recommendation at this point, just a sense that what we’re doing isn’t working well.

Goal for this session is to confirm what information council wants at the later regular meeting and whether they’re comfortable with us targeting the **March 23, 2026** council agenda for formal action.

metadata.title: Council Work Session – Plow Fleet & Truck #23
metadata.location: Brainerd City Hall – Small Conference Room B

artifacts.content:
From: Paul Sandstrom <psandstrom@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Hendrickson <mhendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Kelly Weiss <kweiss@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-27 08:42
Subject: Pinning down date for Truck #23 discussion

Mike,

Following up on our hallway chat yesterday – we need to lock in a requested meeting date for the council item on Truck #23 so I can get it on the drafting calendar.

I tentatively put a note on my end for **Thursday, March 19, 2026**, but that’s just a placeholder until you tell me what works with your schedule and with Melissa’s. Kelly would prefer anything the week of March 9 or March 23 so she can coordinate with the broader CIP update.

In your memo, when you talk about alternatives, don’t forget the human side. You’ve mentioned before that during the January 10–12, 2025 storm you ran the northeast quadrant route yourself for three straight 12‑hour shifts, and by the third night you and Sarah were at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s at about 3:00 a.m. on January 13 because of your chest tightness and blood pressure. That really shows why continuing to cover that route yourself instead of replacing critical trucks isn’t sustainable.

Also, the story about Jacob’s asthma flare‑up at the YMCA on February 9, 2025, when Truck #23 lost hydraulic pressure on Oak Street, is powerful. The way you stayed on the plow while he was scared and then argued at home later really underscores the effects on your family.

Shoot back a quick reply this week with the date you want me to list as the requested meeting date in the staff report.

Thanks,
Paul

metadata.subject: Pinning down date for Truck #23 discussion

artifacts.content:
Title: Drafting Session – Truck #23 Council Memo
Date: 2026-03-05
Time: 1:30 p.m. – 3:00 p.m.
Location: Public Works Garage – Mike’s Office
Attendees: Michael Hendrickson; Kelly Weiss (optional drop‑in)

Description:

Block of time for Michael to finalize the narrative portions of the Truck #23 staff report before sending it to Paul. Kelly suggested we hold this drafting session on **Thursday, March 5, 2026**, so that if we decide to target the **March 30, 2026** regular council meeting, there’s still time for legal review.

Key notes to capture in the memo during this session:

- Summary of Issue: When Truck #23 broke down on Oak Street during the December 5, 2024 storm and I had to call my dad, Tom, at 6:30 a.m. to pick me up near the Crow Wing County Courthouse because the backup unit was already out, it highlighted how close we are to not being able to cover all arterial routes, especially with my reduced overtime availability due to Jacob’s weekly physical therapy at Cuyuna Regional Medical Center on Thursday mornings.

- In terms of effects on others, include how repeated breakdowns in January–February 2025 delayed Emily’s 7:15 a.m. bus to Brainerd High by 25–30 minutes on January 22 and February 5. Her driver, Mark Jensen, told the kids the roads were “borderline unsafe,” and that led to you and Sarah meeting with Assistant Principal Laura Carlson on February 7 to talk about her possibly driving herself on heavy snow days.

- Be honest that we didn’t spend much time looking at other options because this seemed like the easiest way to get the problem off our plate before next winter.

Make sure to leave the actual requested council meeting date blank for now; Paul will confirm which March meeting we’re aiming for after he talks with the Mayor.

From: Kelly Weiss <kweiss@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Hendrickson <mhendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-03-01 14:17
Subject: Council agenda timing – need your preference

Mike,

I’m trying to balance a very full March agenda and need to know what you want listed as the requested meeting date for the Truck #23 item.

Right now, staff has three possibilities floating around:

1) Informal introduction at the work session on **Monday, March 9, 2026**, with a formal action item on **March 23, 2026**.
2) Push everything to **April 6, 2026**, if you feel like you need more time to refine the alternatives.
3) Quick overview under my broader CIP presentation on **March 30, 2026**, and then we bring back a specific authorization later.

Given that you’ve said this request is coming forward now because you didn’t have time earlier in the season to pull everything together and have mostly been handling it informally, I’d lean away from pushing it out again unless you absolutely have to.

Your email last week about being unsure what the best solution is and not having a clear recommendation yet is totally understandable, but council will at least want a sense of direction.

Reply with which of these dates you want me to plug into the “requested meeting date” line in the staff report. If none of them work, propose another date in late March or early April and I’ll see what we can juggle.

Thanks,
Kelly

metadata.subject: Council agenda timing – need your preference

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Hendrickson <mhendrickson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Paul Sandstrom <psandstrom@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Kelly Weiss <kweiss@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-03-03 06:58
Subject: Re: Requested date for Truck #23 item

Paul,

Catching this before I head out to the shop.

After looking at the family calendar (Emily’s ACT prep and Jacob’s hockey tournament in Duluth) and our storm history for mid‑March, I’d prefer that we NOT try to cram the Truck #23 action item into the same week as the school spring concert.

Instead of the tentative **Thursday, March 19, 2026** date you mentioned, could you list **Monday, March 23, 2026** as the requested council meeting date in the staff report? That gives me a little breathing room to clean up the alternatives section and make sure I’ve captured the impacts on my crew and my family.

I’m planning to work in the examples we talked about: the December 5, 2024 breakdown when I had to call my dad Tom for a ride near the courthouse, and the January–February 2025 delays to Emily’s 7:15 a.m. bus route that led to our February 7 meeting with Assistant Principal Carlson. I’ll also include a short note about Jacob’s asthma flare‑up at the YMCA on February 9, 2025, when Truck #23 lost hydraulics on Oak Street and I stayed on the plow instead of going to get him right away.

Honestly, we didn’t spend much time looking at other options because this seemed like the easiest way to get the problem off our plate before next winter, but I’ll frame that carefully.

If March 23 doesn’t work for some reason, my second choice for the requested date would be **April 6, 2026**.

Thanks,
Mike

metadata.subject: Re: Requested date for Truck #23 item
